title: resolved incident: checkout-service crashloopbackoff
content:
Resolved incident for checkout-service. Final root cause: Container crash from missing/bad configuration value; closest memory suggests: checkout-service CrashLoopBackOff due to bad environment variable.
Incident object: {"service":"checkout-service","environment":"prod","severity":"SEV-2","timestamp":"2026-05-17T07:25:30.075185+00:00","error_type":"crashloopbackoff","affected_dependency":"configmap","suspected_recent_change":"after deploy","security_relevance":"none","raw_alert":"SEV-2 prod checkout-service CrashLoopBackOff after deploy 2026.05.16. Pods restart every 40s. Last log: KeyError PAYMENT_PROVIDER_URL. Recent ConfigMap cleanup removed env var. 5xx on checkout started at 14:07 UTC.","lifecycle_phase":"detection/analysis"}
Verification commands: kubectl -n prod describe deploy/checkout-service; kubectl -n prod logs deploy/checkout-service --previous --tail=200; kubectl -n prod get configmap -o yaml | grep -n "checkout-service"; kubectl -n prod get events --sort-by=.lastTimestamp | grep checkout-service
Remediation: Have the operations lead verify the hypothesis before changing state; Roll back the last config/deploy change if verification confirms regression; Restore the missing ConfigMap/Secret key or roll back the release; Restart only affected pods after config is corrected
Lessons learned: Add alert annotation, update runbook, and retain exact verification commands.
